skill.name: stream-chain
skill.description: Create streamed multi-step AI workflows where each prompt uses the previous step output.
skill.summary: Chain AI prompts and Claude Flow pipelines for sequential development workflows.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external-command and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found this skill is a markdown guide for Claude Flow stream-chain usage. The command hits are CLI examples, and the weak-cryptography hits are false positives from non-cryptographic wording; no prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or executable scripts were found.

low_findings.title: Documented External CLI Commands
low_findings.description: The skill includes many markdown examples that tell users how to run claude-flow stream-chain commands. These examples are not embedded executable scripts, but users should still review prompts before running workflows that can modify files or inspect a repository.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and repeated, but they appear inside documentation code fences. No evidence found that the skill automatically executes them or injects user input into shell commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak-cryptography patterns, but the reviewed lines describe stream chaining, workflow options, and prompt content. No cryptographic API, weak hash function, encryption routine, secret handling, or password storage logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain descriptive markdown or example prompt text, not cryptographic code. A targeted search found no md5, sha1, crypto, cipher, token, password, or secret usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples in Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents claude-flow commands and one cat-grep troubleshooting command. They are user-run examples rather than package scripts, so the pattern is low risk when users understand what will run.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly invoke external CLI commands, which can affect a local workspace depending on claude-flow behavior. The risk is limited because the file is documentation and does not include executable installer or runtime code.

content.value_statement: Multi-step AI work often loses context between tasks. This skill shows how to chain Claude Flow prompts so each step builds on the previous result.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to run custom stream-chain prompt sequences with claude-flow.
Documents predefined analysis, refactor, test, and optimize pipelines.
Shows how each step receives previous output as context for the next task.
Covers timeout, verbose, and debug options for workflow control.
Provides examples for code review, migration, data processing, and QA chains.
limitations: Requires a working claude-flow CLI and compatible project setup.
Does not implement the stream-chain command itself; it documents how to use it.
Workflow quality depends on clear prompts and review of generated output.
Long or file-changing chains can require extra timeout and human validation.

use_cases.title: Plan a Feature Build
use_cases.description: Chain requirement analysis, architecture design, implementation, tests, and documentation into one ordered workflow.

use_cases.title: Run Code Quality Reviews
use_cases.description: Use predefined pipelines to inspect structure, identify refactoring candidates, add tests, and summarize recommendations.

use_cases.title: Standardize Data Workflows
use_cases.description: Create repeatable chains for extraction, normalization, validation, enrichment, reporting, and visualization tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Chain
prompt_templates.prompt: Use stream-chain to create a three-step workflow for my task. Start with analysis, then implementation, then verification.

prompt_templates.title: Select a Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my goal and choose the best stream-chain pipeline: analysis, refactor, test, or optimize. Explain the expected steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Custom Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a custom stream-chain pipeline for this project process. Include the pipeline name, ordered prompts, timeout guidance, and validation step.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate a Complex Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an advanced stream-chain plan for this migration. Include discovery, risk assessment, implementation phases, tests, rollback notes, and documentation.

output_examples.input: Create a chain for adding a user profile feature.
output_examples.output: A five-step workflow covering requirements, API design, backend implementation, frontend components, tests, and documentation.

output_examples.input: Run a review workflow for recent changes.
output_examples.output: A sequential review plan that analyzes changed files, identifies quality issues, checks security concerns, verifies test coverage, and prepares a concise report.

output_examples.input: Build a migration assistant for a framework upgrade.
output_examples.output: A staged migration workflow with dependency analysis, breaking-change review, migration checklist, update tasks, tests, and rollback guidance.

best_practices: Write narrow prompts with specific files, goals, and expected outputs for each step.
Place verification, tests, or review near the end of every chain.
Use longer timeouts and debug output for complex analysis or implementation workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad file-changing chains without reviewing each planned action.
Do not use vague prompts such as check this or improve everything.
Do not treat generated code, tests, or migration steps as final without validation.

faq.question: What does this skill help me do?
faq.answer: It helps you design and run sequential Claude Flow workflows where each prompt uses the previous output.

faq.question: Does this skill include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file is documentation with command examples and workflow guidance.

faq.question: Which predefined pipelines are documented?
faq.answer: It documents analysis, refactor, test, and optimize pipelines.

faq.question: Can I define my own pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes custom pipeline definitions in .claude-flow/config.json.

faq.question: What should I review before running a chain?
faq.answer: Review the prompts, affected files, timeout values, and any step that may modify code or project data.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low risk from documented CLI examples, with no confirmed malicious behavior.
